violations.text: An open employees' beverage container was observed stored in a food preparation area. Employees shall not drink from open beverage containers in order to prevent contamination of their hands, the container, and exposed food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/17/2026

Sani bucket was observed in the kitchen hand-washing sink, with a chemical spray bottle hanging on the sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing Items were removed. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/20/2026

Molluscan Shellfish (oysters) located in the walk-in did not have the proper identification tags; tags were placed in filing bag. Molluscan Shellstock tags must remain attached to the container in which the Molluscan Shellstock are received until the container is empty. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/03/2026

Raw animal foods (seafood) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (tofu, cooked tacos) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Staff rearranged refrigerator during inspection. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/20/2026

Guacamole was observed held on ice at 43-53 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC placed quacamole in smaller containers and added ice. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/20/2026

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Various sauces were placed hot in plastic containers in containers greater than four inches deep. Discussed options for cooling with PIC. New Violation. Correct By: 02/03/2026

Utensils (drink strainers) in the bar area are displayed with the lip surface up. Utensils are to be stored so that only the handles are touched by employees and consumers if consumer self-service is provided. New Violation. Correct By: 02/03/2026

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 02/03/2026

A perforated pan with food waste was observed in the hand-washing sink at the bar; a spray bottle was observed handing on the hand-washing sink in the dish room. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Items were removed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/20/2026

Molluscan Shellfish (oysters) located in (walk-in) did not have the proper identification tags. Molluscan Shellstock tags must remain attached to the container in which the Molluscan Shellstock are received until the container is empty. New Violation. Correct By: 02/03/2026
Molluscan Shellfish tags did not have all of the required information. Date last item served was lacking. New Violation. Correct By: 01/20/2026

Raw animal foods (seafood) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (sauces) in the low-boy refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. PIC relocated items. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/20/2026

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (vegetable chopper) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Staff cleaned and sanitized chopper during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/20/2026

Beef was observed held at 113 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Staff instructed to reheat. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/20/2026

Tomatoes were observed held at 64 degrees above the fill line in the prep unit; salsa and guacamole were observed held at 45 degrees Fahrenheit on ice. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC placed guacamole and salsa in smaller containers submerged in ice. Tomatoes were placed in low boy. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/20/2026

The presence of small flies is not controlled as evidenced by small flies around the grease trap/3-bay sink area. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 02/03/2026

Utensils (whisks and spoons) in the clean storage rack are displayed with the lip surface up. Utensils are to be stored so that only the handles are touched by employees and consumers if consumer self-service is provided. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/20/2026
A blender bowl was observed with pooled water. Utensils and equipment must be allowed to air dry. PIC brought to be cleaned and sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/20/2026

The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the quaternary ammonium or chlorine sanitizing solution for the 3-bay sinks or bar dish machines. New Violation. Correct By: 02/03/2026
The facility is lacking thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the dish machine at the kitchen. New Violation. Correct By: 02/03/2026

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. PIC turned off machine to prepare to clean and sanitize. New Violation. Correct By: 08/22/2025

The facility is lacking thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the dish machine at the kitchen. Inspector provided during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/22/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the prep unit gaskets have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 08/29/2025
